label: Funny / Short / 1 child
description: Kid says something hilarious at dinner
transcript: So we're sitting at dinner and um Lila just looks at her broccoli and goes daddy why does this tree taste so angry and I almost spit out my water I could not stop laughing

label: Funny / Medium / 2 children
transcript: Oh my gosh okay so Lila and Noah were um playing in the living room and Lila decided she was a doctor and Noah was the patient and she told him he had quote a serious case of the sillies and the only cure was like ten jumping jacks and then Noah actually did them and she was like okay you're cured that'll be a hundred dollars and he goes I don't have any money and she just goes that's fine we take snacks

label: Funny / Short / 1 child / Garbled audio
description: Speech-to-text didn't do great, lots of gaps
transcript: So Noah he just like  he uh   told me that um  he wants to  you know  marry the  the dog next door because  uh  she's really nice and  has soft ears

label: Sentimental / Medium / 1 child
transcript: Okay so this just happened um I was tucking Lila in and she grabbed my face with both of her little hands and she said mommy when I grow up I want to be just like you but with better hair and I just like I wanted to cry and laugh at the same time and then she asked me to sing the sunshine song one more time and I did and she fell asleep holding my hand and I just sat there for like ten minutes because I didn't want to let go

label: Sentimental / Long / 1 child
description: Parent reflecting on how fast time goes
transcript: I don't even know how to start this um I was going through Noah's closet today trying to find his jacket and I found his little shoes from when he was like one year old and I just held them and they're so small I mean I know everyone says this but like they really are so tiny and I remember when he could barely walk and he would just toddle around in those shoes and now he's running and jumping and he lost his first tooth last week and I just feel like I blinked and he grew up so fast and I keep telling myself to slow down and notice these moments but time just keeps going you know and I'm really glad I'm recording this because I don't want to forget what this feels like right now just sitting here holding his little shoes

label: Sentimental / Short / 1 child
transcript: Lila just came up to me totally unprompted and said I love you bigger than the whole sky and then just walked away like it was nothing

label: Milestone / Short / 1 child
description: First bike ride without training wheels
transcript: Noah just rode his bike without training wheels for the first time like he actually did it he was so scared at first but then he just went and he was screaming I'm doing it I'm doing it and I was running behind him crying

label: Milestone / Medium / 1 child
transcript: So today was Lila's first day of preschool and um I thought I was ready but I was not ready at all she was totally fine she walked right in and didn't even look back and I was like standing in the parking lot like a mess you know and the other parents were kind of laughing because we were all crying and our kids were fine and when I picked her up she told me she made a friend named Marcus and they built a tower together and she said it was the best day ever and I was like okay she's going to be just fine it's me who's not fine

label: Everyday / Medium / 1 child
transcript: Nothing major happened today but we made cookies together me and Noah and he was so serious about it like he was reading the recipe to me even though he can't really read yet he was just kind of making it up and he told me we needed a cup of love and I was like okay where do I find that and he pointed at his chest and said right here mom and then he ate half the chocolate chips before they made it into the batter

label: Everyday / Short / 2 children
description: Kids playing together nicely for once
transcript: I just want to remember this um Lila and Noah are sitting on the floor right now sharing crayons and like actually getting along and Noah keeps handing Lila the colors she asks for and she's saying thank you every time and it's so peaceful I almost don't want to breathe

label: Everyday / Long / 1 child / Rambling
description: Parent rambling about a park trip with lots of tangents
transcript: Okay so we went to the park today and I forgot to bring snacks which was like a huge mistake because Noah gets so hangry when he doesn't eat anyway we got there and he immediately ran to the swings and there was this other kid there who I think was maybe like three or four and they just started playing together like they'd known each other forever and the other kid's mom and I were talking and she was really nice actually she just moved here from Portland anyway Noah went down the big slide by himself which he's never done before and he was so proud and then we saw a caterpillar on the bench and he wanted to take it home obviously and I said no and he was upset for like two seconds and then he forgot about it because he saw a dog and the dog was really cute actually it was like a golden retriever mix or something anyway we stayed for like an hour and a half and he fell asleep in the car on the way home

label: Bittersweet / Medium / 1 child
transcript: So Lila told me tonight that she doesn't need me to walk her to her room anymore she said I'm a big girl now I can do it myself and I said okay that's great honey and she just walked down the hall by herself and I stood there like an idiot because like I know this is a good thing right she's growing up she's becoming independent but that walk to her room was like our thing you know I would carry her and she would point at things on the wall and ask me questions and now she just walks and I know I should be proud and I am but I'm also just a little bit sad

label: Mixed tone / Long / 2 children
transcript: Oh man this morning was chaos um so Lila woke up at like five thirty because she had a bad dream and she crawled into our bed and then Noah heard her and he came in too so now we've got four people in a queen size bed and nobody's sleeping and then Lila says she's hungry so I go make pancakes and Noah wants to help but he spills the milk everywhere and I mean everywhere and I'm trying not to get frustrated because it's early and I haven't had coffee yet and then Lila comes in and she's wearing her princess dress over her pajamas and she goes I'm ready for the ball and Noah still has milk all over his hands and he gives her a hug and gets milk all over her princess dress and she doesn't even care she just hugs him back and they're both laughing and I'm standing there in the kitchen covered in pancake batter and milk and honestly it was perfect I wouldn't trade it for anything

label: Quotable / Short / 1 child
transcript: Noah just asked me um why do we have to sleep every night and I said because our bodies need rest and he thought about it for a second and said but what if my dreams are better than the rest and I was like okay tiny philosopher settle down

label: Mundane magic / Medium / 1 child
description: Finding beauty in a totally ordinary moment
transcript: We were just driving home from the grocery store nothing special and the sun was coming through the window and Lila was in her car seat and she started singing to herself she was just making up a song and it wasn't like a real song it was just sort of like today we got bananas and the sun is warm and I love my mommy and she had no idea I was listening and I just looked at her in the rearview mirror and her little face was all lit up by the sun and she was just so happy and content just making up her little song about bananas and I thought this is it this is the whole thing right here
